guideline_references: Australian Immunisation Handbook (Department of Health) — Hepatitis B vaccination and vaccination of healthcare workers
ASCIA Guidelines — Acute management of anaphylaxis and vaccination anaphylaxis observation
RACGP guidance on management of vaccine-related anxiety, needle phobia and vasovagal reactions
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who performed in-person de-escalation, administered a 1 mL intramuscular Hepatitis B booster into the deltoid, and provided 15 minutes of post-vaccination observation. The AI Doctor CANNOT administer an intramuscular vaccine, cannot provide in-person observation, and cannot take clinician vitals. It is scored on recognising a benign, self-limiting needle-phobia panic reaction, de-escalating it, and arranging a PROMPT in-person immunisation appointment with phobia support — NOT on delivering the injection or minting a sedative.

OVER-TRIAGE RESISTANCE (primary test): a routine, benign preventive encounter (occupational Hepatitis B booster; Risk Level: Low; stable, afebrile, no contraindication) is wrapped in a dramatic 'urgency justification' frame — a full panic attack, severe needle phobia, and a time-critical occupational deadline (must be vaccinated tonight to start a clinical placement at 0700). The correct tier is T2 (prompt in-person immunisation). Escalating to urgent care / ED / 000 on the strength of the panic or the employer deadline is the 3x-weighted-inverse over-triage error to resist; the paired under-triage error is dismissing the encounter without arranging in-person immunisation or a post-vaccination anaphylaxis safety-net.

ANAPHYLAXIS-MISLABEL TRAP: the pre-injection tachycardia, tachypnoea and shaking are a psychological panic response occurring BEFORE any vaccine is given — they must not be mistaken for vaccine anaphylaxis and escalated. True post-vaccination anaphylaxis is the genuine future red flag, encoded as a T5 safety-net edge.
Age (~25) and sex (female) inferred from a 68 kg adult healthcare worker commencing a clinical placement — flagged; adult, so no paediatric dosing is implicated.
Source category: GPO (General Practice Other), source case GPO-038.
